title: taraxacum lacinulatum
intro: Taraxacum lacinulatum, also known as the variable dandelion or cut-leaf dandelion, is a plant species in the sunflower family that is characterized by its distinctive cut or lobed leaves. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Taraxacum lacinulatum include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestive health, as well as its antioxidant properties which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Taraxacum lacinulatum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can be used to support liver and kidney function, as well as to aid in the treatment of various digestive disorders.
